body.title: Instrument memory traversal timing to set calving threshold
body.result: instance.traversal_timing table (m060) + traverse_from/interpretive_traverse instrumented with daemon-thread writes; 3 integration tests pass
body.intention: I intend that the calving threshold is set by observed search time, not an arbitrary row count.
body.description:
The live calving threshold (IGOR_CALVING_THRESHOLD=1000) was set without timing data. The real signal should be search time. Add timing instrumentation to cortex.traverse_from() and traverse_interpretive() — log (tree_name, node_count, search_time_ms) to a new instance.traversal_timing table. After 2 weeks of live data, surface the time-vs-size curve to inform the threshold decision.

**Affected files:** devices/igor/memory/cortex.py (timing wrapper on traverse_from + traverse_interpretive; migration: CREATE TABLE instance.traversal_timing(id SERIAL, tree_name TEXT, node_count INT, search_time_ms REAL, recorded_at TIMESTAMPTZ))
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; timing overhead < 1% of traversal time; graceful degradation (timing failure never blocks traversal); log at DEBUG level
**Scope boundary:** IN: timing instrumentation, traversal_timing table. OUT: changing IGOR_CALVING_THRESHOLD (follow-on ticket once data is reviewed)
**Completion criteria:** After 100 traversal calls, SELECT COUNT(*) FROM instance.traversal_timing > 0; rows show node_count and search_time_ms; a threshold recommendation is filed as a follow-on S ticket within 2 weeks.
